abstract: While channel and spatial attention mechanisms have demonstrated remarkable success in generating highly discriminative feature representations across various computer vision tasks, current methods that rely on channel dimensionality reduction often suffer from information loss, hindering the extraction of robust deep visual representations. In this paper, we introduce an Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) module designed to mitigate these drawbacks. To preserve per-channel information while minimizing computational overhead, our approach reshapes a portion of the channels into batch dimensions and partitions the channel space into multiple sub-features. This ensures that spatial semantic features are evenly distributed within each group. Furthermore, beyond encoding global information to recalibrate channel-wise weights in parallel branches, we utilize cross-dimensional interaction to aggregate parallel branch outputs, effectively capturing pixel-level pairwise relationships. Extensive ablation studies and performance evaluations on image classification and object detection benchmarks—including CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO, and VisDrone2019—demonstrate that EMA consistently outperforms existing attention mechanisms in both efficiency and accuracy.
